result:
1. Rate limiter `multi.exec()` destructure bug — Section 4 code. `ioredis` returns `[[err, val], ...]` tuples from `exec()`. `const [count] = await multi.exec()` binds `count` to `[null, <int>]`, so `count > limit` compares array-to-number (coerces, but breaks on `null` errors). Fix: `const [[, count]] = await multi.exec()` and check the err slot.

2. Documented rate-limit key still missing region prefix — Section 4 shows `rl:{ip}:{route_prefix}:{minute_bucket}`, but Section 10 (2025-12 SEV2) says region prefix was added post-incident. Either handbook is stale or the regression returned. Fix: `rl:{region}:{ip}:{route_prefix}:{minute_bucket}` and verify in code/tests.

3. JWT exp/nbf skew logic is self-contradictory — Section 5. "exp checked against `Date.now() + skewMs`" would *extend* validity; next sentence rejects when `exp < Date.now() - skewMs` which *shortens* it. Only one of these is right for each of exp/nbf (reject if `exp < now - skew`; reject if `nbf > now + skew`). Clarify and add tests for both boundaries.

4. "Sliding window" is actually fixed-bucket — Section 4. `Math.floor(Date.now()/60000)` is a fixed minute bucket, permitting ~2× burst across the boundary. Either rename to "fixed window" or switch to a real sliding-window (two-bucket weighted, or sorted-set ZREMRANGEBYSCORE).

5. Per-IP only limits → brute-force still viable via IP rotation post-2025-09 fix — Section 10 2025-09 RCA only hardened IP derivation; attacker moving across residential/NAT IPs still defeats throttling. Add a per-`sub`/per-account failed-auth counter keyed on `X-Internal-User-Id` (or username pre-auth) with lockout.

6. Missing `Retry-After` HTTP header on 429 — Section 4 emits `retry_after` only in JSON body. Standards-compliant clients and CDNs key off the header. Add `res.setHeader('Retry-After', secondsUntilNextBucket())`.

7. Header sanitization is implicit denylist — Section 2 rule 1 lists specific headers to strip. Any future internal header (`X-Internal-*`, `X-Forwarded-*`, `X-Real-*`) added downstream is trusted until someone remembers to update the list. Switch to allowlist: strip any `X-Internal-*` and `X-Forwarded-*` unconditionally at ingress; add only what the pipeline re-sets after auth.

8. JWKS stale-while-revalidate extends poisoning blast radius — Section 2 rule 4 + 2025-06 SEV1. 16-min soft TTL with SWR means a poisoned key set keeps serving beyond the 15-min refresh, compounding the cache-flush recovery. Either drop SWR for JWKS (refresh-ahead only) or gate it behind a signature/kid sanity check before promotion.

9. `jti` replay detection punted to downstream with no enforcement — Section 5. "Responsibility of downstream services that care" means services that don't explicitly opt in are vulnerable to token replay within `exp - now`. Track which downstreams implement it, or do edge-side jti blocklist for sensitive routes.

10. Auto-rollback on "SEV1 ANY HOUR" thrashes on external-cause incidents — Section 6. A downstream `auth-svc` JWKS outage (cf. 2025-06) or Redis flap triggers SEV1 auto-rollback, which does nothing useful and may interrupt an in-progress fix. Gate auto-rollback on "error class originated in atlas-edge" (deploy-correlated signals: new error signatures, timing aligned with sync event), not on raw SEV1.

11. Custom lint rule `no-unbounded-redis-key` likely misses `MULTI` patterns — Section 11. Rule matches `redis.incr` but Section 4 uses `multi.incr` / `multi.expire` on a pipeline object; name resolution differs. Verify the AST matcher covers `.multi()` chains and both `redis.*` and `Pipeline.*` receivers, or the `EXPIRE` invariant is silently unenforceable for the very pattern the code recommends.

12. PII-redaction verification runs staging-only — Section 2 rule 5 + Section 9. Hourly `pii-leak-detector` on staging won't catch a production-only log path (e.g. a route flagged in `always_trace_routes.json` with debug fields). Run the detector against a sampled tail of Loki prod logs too.
